Name: hardware_faithful_optimization
Title: Hardware-Faithful Optimization of Machine Learning Inference on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: By optimizing machine learning inference kernels for commodity GPUs and evaluating their performance using hardware-faithful metrics, we can achieve significant improvements in wall-clock latency and throughput without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works such as Spectral Latent Steering and Spectral Quantization Routing have proposed various methods for optimizing machine learning inference, but they often rely on theoretical bounds and lack hardware-faithful evaluation. Our approach focuses on optimizing inference kernels for commodity GPUs and evaluating their performance using metrics such as wall-clock latency, throughput, and energy per token.
Abstract: This proposal presents a novel approach to optimizing machine learning inference on commodity GPUs. By focusing on kernel-level throughput engineering and hardware-faithful evaluation, we aim to achieve significant improvements in wall-clock latency and throughput without sacrificing accuracy. Our approach involves optimizing fused CUDA/Triton kernels, memory-bandwidth utilization, KV-cache layout, and tensor-core occupancy, and evaluating their performance using metrics such as wall-clock latency, throughput, and energy per token. We will conduct experiments on a variety of machine learning models and datasets, and report results using hardware-faithful metrics.
Experiments: Optimize fused CUDA/Triton kernels for machine learning inference on commodity GPUs
Evaluate the performance of optimized kernels using hardware-faithful metrics such as wall-clock latency, throughput, and energy per token
Conduct experiments on a variety of machine learning models and datasets, including image classification, natural language processing, and speech recognition
Report results using hardware-faithful metrics, including p50/p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy per token
Risk Factors and Limitations: The optimization process may require significant computational resources and expertise in CUDA/Triton kernel optimization
The evaluation metrics used may not capture all aspects of system performance, and additional metrics may be needed to fully understand the impact of the optimizations
The results may not generalize to all types of machine learning models and datasets, and additional experiments may be needed to fully understand the applicability of the approach
